title: How do I remove the .svg fox image from error pages?
content: The recent update 149.0 introduced a new look to the error pages. Every single time the requested URL does not exist, a page's servers are down, or your internet is turned off, it shows an image of a fox biting a power plug. The image is no-connection.svg located in omni.ja (chrome://global/skin/illustrations/no-connection.svg). Such a location rooted in the files cannot be zapped by uBlock Origin. I tried to replace the image inside omni.ja using 7-zip, but it can't do so because the Mozilla Firefox folder is Read-Only, and keeps turning so despite me unchecking the Read-Only checkbox in properties. I tried looking for a variable in about:config, toggled svg.disabled from false to true, it didn't help. My goal is so that only text appears on error pages, and no images, like it was in the previous update. Ideally I would like to have the option to completely eliminate corporate artwork from my copy of Firefox. Help would be very much appreciated, because Firefox is my favourite browser by far
creator: browserdinosaur

title: firefox corrompendo imagens em artefatos / Firefox corrupting images in artifacts
content:
PT

Sempre que colo uma imagem dentro do navegador, assim que a imagem é carregada ela corrompe e fica com linhas de artefato. Tentei com imagens tiradas de prints usando Win + Shift + S e também usando a própria ferramenta de captura do Firefox, mas sempre que colo essa imagem em algum lugar dentro do navegador o problema ocorre.

o problema não ocorre com outros navegadores como edge.

EN

Whenever I paste an image into the browser, as soon as the image loads, it becomes corrupted and shows artifact lines. I tried images taken from screenshots using Win + Shift + S, as well as using Firefox's built-in screenshot tool, but whenever I paste the image anywhere inside the browser, the problem occurs.

The problem does not occur with other browsers like Edge

title: Update Firefox - as system account in Task scheduler
content:
I would like to setup Firefox so it checks for updates and installs them during maintenance windows (overnight) and runs the updates as 'system' account. This is how Chrome and Edge handle updates.

I tried using the following GPO settings

Application Autoupdate Enabled Background updater Enabled Disable Firefox Studies Enabled Disable Telemetry Enabled Disable Update Disabled Don't Check Default Browser Enabled

This seems to create Schedule tasks in Windows task scheduler, but the tasks are running as previously logged on user accounts. which doesn't help us in this particular environment.

I know we can manually push out the latest version via creating new MCM application pushes but the amount of updates that are coming down is a labor intensive exercise and we don't have the budget for PatchMyPC

Please post any thoughts!
